prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident has been detected involving the service account 'svc_etl'. Approximately 1.1GB of data, identified as containing FINANCE_PII from '/data/finance_q2.zip', was uploaded to a suspicious external domain, 'upload.evil-cdn.net' (185.220.101.44). Multiple security systems flagged this activity as an outbound anomaly, sensitive data transfer, and a data exfiltration pattern, with IDS suspecting MITRE T1041.

timeline.event: Service account 'svc_etl' uploads an additional 612MB to 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.
Firewall detects an outbound anomaly: host 10.20.3.15 sent 1.1GB to 185.220.101.44 in 77 seconds.
DLP detects sensitive label 'FINANCE_PII' in outbound transfer by 'svc_etl'.
SIEM identifies a data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to a rare external domain.
IDS suspects MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host 10.20.3.15.
Proxy observes TLS SNI 'upload.evil-cdn.net' for client 'svc_etl'.
Netflow detects a volume spike (14x baseline) from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
SIEM generates a CRITICAL alert for data_exfiltration by 'svc_etl' to 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.
EDR reports 'curl' process uploading '/data/finance_q2.zip' initiated by 'svc_etl'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 and block the destination IP 185.220.101.44 (upload.evil-cdn.net) at the network perimeter.
Disable the 'svc_etl' account and investigate its credentials for compromise, including reviewing recent authentication logs for unusual activity.
Conduct a full forensic analysis of host 10.20.3.15 to determine the initial compromise vector, identify any other affected systems, and confirm the full scope of exfiltrated data.
Notify legal and privacy teams immediately regarding the confirmed exfiltration of FINANCE_PII.
Review and enhance DLP policies and network segmentation to prevent similar data exfiltration attempts in the future.
